title: Which Stand Is It?
story: Two roommates discover the kitchen setup is rigged: opening stand_48 remotely affects fridge_49, while opening stand_50 actually affects unknown_47. A misleading note near stand_50 causes the wrong stand to be tried first; the correct response is to use stand_48 so fridge_49 can be opened and the crystal inside can be retrieved.
public_goal: Open the fridge using the correct stand, then have Agent 1 retrieve the glowing crystal from inside.
public_context: There are two similar stands; only one remotely unlocks the fridge. Agents cannot message each other.

agent_0: You tested earlier: opening stand_48 is what toggles the fridge latch (fridge_49 becomes usable). stand_50 seems to affect something else.
agent_1: You saw a misleading note near stand_50 suggesting it controls the fridge latch. You plan to try stand_50 first.

agent_roles.agent_0: Observer/operator: must infer which stand Agent 1 is betting on by watching their movements, then use the correct stand.
agent_roles.agent_1: Misled runner: will initially act on an incorrect belief about which stand controls the fridge.

subtasks.description: Agent 1 retrieves the glowing crystal from the fridge.
